questions.text: UML sequence diagram is one of diagrams that model:
questions.explanation: Sequence diagrams are dynamic modeling diagrams in UML that describe how objects interact in a specific order over time.

questions.text: Requirement can be expressed as scenarios:
questions.explanation: Scenarios and Use Cases are common ways to express functional requirements by describing user interactions with the system.

questions.text: Reverse engineering is the process of extracting code from software model:

choices.label: D
choices.text: True, but only for legacy systems

questions.explanation: Reverse engineering is the process of extracting high-level design models and documentation from existing source code, not the other way around (which is Forward Engineering).

questions.text: Testing a complete version of the software before it is released to users is called:
questions.explanation: Release testing is the process of testing a particular release of a system that is intended for use outside of the development team.

questions.text: The software evolution process depends on which of the following factors?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The type of software being maintained

choices.label: C
choices.text: The skills and experience of the people involved

questions.explanation: Software evolution is a complex process influenced by the nature of the software, the organizational processes, and the human resources available.

questions.text: Objects are instances from classes:
questions.explanation: In object-oriented programming, a class is a blueprint, and an object is a specific instance of that blueprint.

questions.text: Which of the following is best developed using prototypes?
questions.explanation: Business systems with heavy user interaction benefit most from prototyping to refine requirements. Safety-critical systems require formal verification where prototyping might not be sufficient for the core logic.

questions.text: The benefits from reengineering rather than developing a new system include:
questions.explanation: Reengineering an existing system is generally lower risk than building from scratch because the core functionality and business rules are already established and proven.

questions.text: The object-oriented design process includes which of the following activities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Define the context and external interaction with system

questions.explanation: OOD involves understanding the system context, modeling the internal architecture and objects, and defining the interfaces through which objects interact.

questions.text: The ability to write a set of tests that can demonstrate that the delivered system meets each specification is called:
questions.explanation: Verifiability means that requirements should be written so that they can be objectively tested to prove the system meets them.

questions.text: Safety, reliability, and usability can be checked using inspections:
questions.explanation: Inspection is a static analysis technique where people review code or documents to find errors, including violations of safety, reliability, or usability standards.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a non-functional requirement?
questions.explanation: Non-functional requirements define system attributes like performance, usability, and constraints (standards, languages), rather than specific behaviors.

questions.text: A software system is developed as a series of versions in the:
questions.explanation: The incremental approach involves developing the system in stages (increments), resulting in successive versions with increasing functionality.

questions.text: Testing individual program parts or object classes is called:
questions.explanation: Unit testing focuses on testing the smallest testable parts of an application, such as functions, methods, or classes, in isolation.

questions.text: A release software is made available to users to allow them to experiment and to raise problems; this process is called:
questions.explanation: Beta testing is when a system is released to a group of potential customers for testing in their own environment.

questions.text: Requirement engineering is:
questions.explanation: Requirement engineering typically involves repeated cycles of elicitation, analysis, and validation (iteration) to ensure accuracy.

questions.text: In a software process, the software architectural model is considered a:
questions.explanation: An architectural model is a tangible output or artifact (Deliverable) resulting from the design activities in the software process.

questions.text: Which of the following activities is done to be sure that the requirements can be checked?
questions.explanation: Verifiability checking ensures that every requirement is stated in such a way that it can be tested or verified.

questions.text: Which of the following relationship models represent the statement: 'book contains 300 pages'?
questions.explanation: Aggregation represents a 'part-of' or 'contains' relationship where one object is composed of others.

questions.text: 'Requirement confusion' is a primary disadvantage of which approach for writing system specifications?
questions.explanation: Natural language is often ambiguous and imprecise, leading to requirements confusion and misinterpretation.

questions.text: "The Bank system should not allow users to send emails" is an example of:
questions.explanation: Functional requirements describe what the system should or should not do. A specific restriction on system behavior like 'not allowing users to send emails' is a functional constraint.

questions.text: Most of the maintenance cost is spent on:
questions.explanation: Studies in software engineering (like those by Lientz and Swanson) show that perfective maintenance—adding new features and responding to new user requirements—consumes the largest portion of maintenance budgets, often over 50%.

questions.text: A ______ is a structured set of activities required to develop a software system:
questions.explanation: A software process is defined as the sequence of activities that leads to the production of a software product.

questions.text: The aggregation relationship is represented in the UML notation by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Arrow pointed to the whole class

choices.label: B
choices.text: Arrow pointed to the part class

choices.label: C
choices.text: Open diamond connected to the part class

choices.label: D
choices.text: Open diamond connected to the whole class

questions.explanation: In UML, aggregation is represented by an open (hollow) diamond shape on the side of the container (the 'whole' class).

questions.text: When the number of requests for corrective maintenance increases, it is an indication for declining in maintainability.
questions.explanation: A high frequency of bugs requiring corrective maintenance usually suggests that the system's internal structure is fragile and hard to maintain.

questions.text: The X-axis of the sequence diagram represents:
questions.explanation: In a UML sequence diagram, the horizontal axis (X-axis) lists the objects or participants involved, while the vertical axis (Y-axis) represents time.

questions.text: The ______ diagram models the other systems in the environment.
questions.explanation: A context diagram (or system context model) shows the boundaries of the system and its relationship with external systems and entities in its environment.

questions.text: Random data is used to test software application:
questions.explanation: Random testing (also known as Fuzzing) is a common technique where invalid, unexpected, or random data is provided as input to a software program.

questions.text: Removing a duplicate code in the program is an example for:
questions.explanation: Refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. Eliminating duplicated code is one of the most common refactoring tasks.

questions.text: Every aggregation relationship is an association:
questions.explanation: Aggregation is a specialized form of Association in UML. Therefore, all aggregations are associations, but not all associations are aggregations.

questions.text: Software design and implementation are interleaved activities:
questions.explanation: In modern software development models (like Agile or Evolutionary Development), design and implementation frequently overlap as feedback from implementation informs the design.

questions.text: Any constraint on the development process is a:
questions.explanation: Organizational requirements include process requirements which specify the standards, languages, or development methods to be used by the organization.

questions.text: Which of the following relationships models the following statement "parrots are birds":
questions.explanation: The 'is-a' relationship is modeled by Generalization (Inheritance) in UML, where 'Parrot' is a subclass of 'Bird'.

questions.text: In ______ you model other systems in the environment of the system being developed:
questions.explanation: The system context model defines the boundary between the system and its environment, showing external entities and systems.

questions.text: To update the system to reflect changes in the operating system is:
questions.explanation: Adaptive maintenance is performed to keep the software usable in a changed or changing environment, such as a new OS version or hardware platform.

questions.text: Code bad smells are bugs that must be fixed.
questions.explanation: Code smells are not bugs. They are surface indications of deeper design or implementation problems that may increase the likelihood of future bugs or make maintenance harder.

questions.text: Understand and define the context and the external interaction with the system is an activity of:
questions.explanation: Context modeling and defining external interactions are critical steps in Requirements Engineering to establish system boundaries.

questions.text: In automated software testing, the checking of program output with the expected result is called:
questions.explanation: In unit testing frameworks, an 'assertion' is the piece of code that compares the actual result from the test to the expected value.

questions.text: Which of these factors is a primary driver for perfective maintenance?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Critical bugs in the source code

questions.explanation: Perfective maintenance involves modifying the software to provide new functionalities or enhancements requested by users after the system has been in operation.

questions.text: Software testing is conducted to prove the absence of errors.
questions.explanation: Software testing can only show the presence of errors, not their absence. As noted by Dijkstra, testing is a process of finding bugs, but a lack of found bugs does not guarantee that none exist.

questions.text: Refactoring is part of the reengineering process.
questions.explanation: Software reengineering is the examination and alteration of a system to reconstitute it in a new form. Refactoring, which involves restructuring existing code to improve its internal qualities without changing its external behavior, is a standard activity performed during the restructuring phase of software reengineering.

questions.text: Which of the following statements are true about reengineering and refactoring?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both happen throughout the development and maintenance phases.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reengineering helps avoid the structure and code modification

questions.explanation: Refactoring is a continuous improvement of code during development and maintenance. While reengineering is often a larger-scale project, both processes are integral parts of the software lifecycle aimed at maintaining and improving system quality.

questions.text: When software users and software developers collaborate to test a software application at the software developer site, it is called:
questions.explanation: **Alpha testing** is simulated or actual operational testing by potential users/customers or an independent test team at the developers' site. **Beta testing** comes after alpha and is performed by the users at their own sites.

questions.text: The Composition relationship is represented in the UML notation by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Black diamond connected to the Whole Class

choices.label: B
choices.text: Black diamond connected to the Part Class

choices.label: C
choices.text: White diamond connected to the Whole Class

choices.label: D
choices.text: White diamond connected to the Part Class

questions.explanation: In UML, **Composition** (strong ownership) is depicted as a filled (black) diamond on the side of the container class (the 'Whole'). **Aggregation** (weak ownership) is depicted by a hollow (white) diamond.

questions.text: The description (flow of events) section in the UML Use case model helps in designing:
questions.explanation: The flow of events (scenarios) helps developers identify participating objects (Class diagrams), the logic of their interaction (Sequence Diagrams), and the structural relationships between them (Associations).

questions.text: When the development team has used an agile approach but the evolution team is unfamiliar with agile methods and prefer a plan-based approach, this problem is called:
questions.explanation: The **handover problem** occurs during the transition phase between development and maintenance teams, especially when there is a mismatch in methodologies (e.g., Agile vs. Plan-based) and documentation expectations.

questions.text: Which phases may not exist in the emergency repair process?
questions.explanation: During emergency repairs, time is critical. Developers typically bypass formal requirements analysis and updating design models (like UML) to focus solely on analyzing and modifying the source code to resolve the issue.

questions.text: Refactoring aims at all of the following, except:
questions.explanation: **Refactoring** is specifically defined as the process of changing a software system to improve its internal structure without changing its external behavior. Adding functionality changes behavior and is therefore not refactoring.

questions.text: True or False: Random data is used to test software applications.
questions.explanation: This is true. Techniques such as **Random Testing** and **Fuzzing** involve using randomly generated inputs to test the robustness and security of a software application.

questions.text: What are the primary notations of a UML use case diagram?
questions.explanation: A Use Case diagram consists of **Actors** (the people or systems using the software) and **Use Cases** (the discrete tasks or goals the system performs for those actors).

questions.text: When comparing software inspection and software testing, which statement is generally true regarding the effort required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The effort to perform software inspection is less than software testing

choices.label: B
choices.text: The effort to perform software testing is more than software inspection

choices.label: C
choices.text: The effort to perform software inspection is equal to the effort to perform software testing

questions.explanation: Generally, software testing involves significantly more effort due to the need for test planning, environment configuration, test data generation, and repeated execution (regression), compared to a static review/inspection process.

questions.text: One of the following testing methods focuses on testing the functionality of objects or methods.
questions.explanation: Unit testing is the process of testing individual components (units) of a software application, such as functions, objects, or methods, in isolation to ensure they work as intended.

questions.text: Which of the following relationships models the following statement: The Software Engineering department offers many courses.
questions.explanation: An association is a structural relationship that specifies that objects of one element are connected to objects of another. 'Offers' describes a standard relationship between a department and courses.

questions.text: Testing the software with real data in the real customer environment is known as __________.
questions.explanation: Beta testing is conducted by end-users in their own environment using real data to identify any remaining issues before the final release.

questions.text: A System Context model is an interaction model?
questions.explanation: Context models are structural models that show the system and its environment. Interaction models, like Use Case diagrams or Sequence diagrams, show how the system interacts with its environment or how components within the system interact.

questions.text: The Y-axis of the sequence diagram represents:
questions.explanation: In a UML sequence diagram, objects are typically arrayed along the X-axis (horizontal), and time progresses down the Y-axis (vertical).

questions.text: In the software evolution process, assessing how much of the system is affected by the change and how much it might cost to implement the change is called:
questions.explanation: The process described is called 'Impact Analysis'. Since Impact Analysis is not listed as a specific option, 'None of the above' is the correct choice.

questions.text: Modelling the context of the system is a modeling from:
questions.explanation: Context models are used to illustrate the operational context of a system – they show what lies outside the system boundaries from an external perspective.

questions.text: ("Are we building the right product") is what ___________ means:
questions.explanation: Validation ensures that the software meets the user's requirements (building the right product), while Verification ensures that the software is built according to the specification (building the product right).

questions.text: In automated software testing, the input and the expected output are used to initialize a test case in ___________ part.
questions.explanation: In most automated testing frameworks, the 'Setup' or 'Initialization' phase is where inputs are prepared. Expected outputs are typically used in the 'Assertion' phase for comparison. The specific act of initializing a test data structure with both values doesn't fit the standard naming of the options provided (Assertion, Joint, Call).

questions.text: Increasing development cost typically results in which of the following outcomes in software engineering?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reduces maintenance cost and potentially decreases total software cost

questions.explanation: In software engineering, spending more during the development phase (e.g., better design, more rigorous testing, higher quality code) generally leads to significantly lower maintenance costs. If the reduction in maintenance cost is greater than the increase in development cost, the total lifecycle cost of the software decreases.

questions.text: The correct sequence of software testing phases is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Component Testing -> Acceptance Testing -> Unit Testing -> System Testing

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unit Testing -> Component Testing -> System Testing -> Acceptance Testing

choices.label: C
choices.text: Unit Testing -> Component Testing -> Acceptance Testing -> System Testing

choices.label: D
choices.text: Component Testing -> Unit Testing -> System Testing -> Acceptance Testing

questions.explanation: Testing usually follows a bottom-up hierarchy: first, individual units are tested (**Unit Testing**), then grouped into modules (**Component Testing**), then the entire integrated software is tested (**System Testing**), and finally, it is validated against user requirements (**Acceptance Testing**).

questions.text: Which of the following UML diagrams is explicitly time-oriented?
questions.explanation: A **Sequence diagram** is time-oriented because its vertical axis (lifelines) explicitly represents the passage of time, showing the chronological order of messages exchanged between objects.

questions.text: Identifying the object classes in a software system is generally considered to be:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The easiest part of object-oriented design

choices.label: B
choices.text: The difficult part of object-oriented design

choices.label: C
choices.text: An automated process in object-oriented design

questions.explanation: Class identification is often the most difficult and creative part of OOD because there is no single algorithmic way to do it correctly. It requires deep analysis of the problem domain and architectural intuition.

questions.text: Which of the following factors affect(s) software maintenance cost?
questions.explanation: Maintenance costs are influenced by technical factors (program age, code quality/structure), organizational factors (team turnover, staff expertise), and external factors (contractual obligations to the customer).

questions.text: Automated testing is utilized in which of the following testing types?
questions.explanation: In modern software development (CI/CD), automated testing is integrated into almost every stage, from low-level unit tests during development to high-level automated acceptance and regression testing during release.

questions.text: Which of the following statements regarding the software 'servicing' phase is true?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The software is first used and usually faces many changes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The software is stable and only undergoes minor bug fixes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The servicing phase precedes the evolution phase.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is the phase where the software is first developed.

questions.explanation: In software evolution models, the 'evolution' phase involves major functional changes. The 'servicing' phase comes later, when the software is mature; at this point, changes are typically limited to minor bug fixes or small patches as the software approaches retirement.

questions.text: Which of the following phases may NOT exist in an emergency repair process?
questions.explanation: During an emergency repair, formal requirements analysis is often bypassed to save time and address the critical issue immediately. The process jumps straight to code analysis and fix implementation.

questions.text: In a UML sequence diagram, the horizontal (X) axis represents:
questions.explanation: In standard sequence diagram notation, objects or lifelines are placed along the X-axis (horizontally), while time proceeds downward along the Y-axis (vertically).

questions.text: Given a field that accepts numbers from 1 to 100, which of the following is a correct set of test inputs for Equivalence Partitioning testing?

choices.label: D
choices.text: All of the mentioned are used to test different partitions

questions.explanation: Equivalence partitioning requires testing a representative value from each partition. For the range 1-100, you have one valid partition (1 to 100) and two invalid partitions (values < 1 and values > 100). Therefore, inputs from all three partitions are necessary for the technique.

questions.text: In software inspections, the team can analyze which of the following system representations?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Software Architecture and Design Models (e.g., Class/Sequence diagrams)

questions.explanation: Software inspections are a static verification technique that can be applied to any formal representation of the system, including requirements, architecture, design diagrams, and source code.

questions.text: In software evolution activity, during the __________, one or all proposed changes (fault repair, platform adaptation, and system enhancement) are considered for inclusion in the next version of the system.
questions.explanation: Release planning is the stage in software evolution where the team decides which specific changes and improvements will be included in the upcoming release, balancing cost and urgency.

questions.text: The following are standard testing stages EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Standard stages of testing typically include Development testing (by developers), Release testing (by a QA team), and User testing (by customers). 'Repair testing' is not a recognized standalone testing stage in standard SE models.

questions.text: The __________ means that you expect the system to perform correctly using a given set of test cases that reflect the system's expected use.
questions.explanation: Validation testing is designed to show that the system meets its requirements and performs as the user expects. This is distinct from defect testing, which aims to find bugs.

questions.text: True or False: The evolution process of software systems is highly dependent on the skills and expertise of evolution teams.
questions.explanation: Because legacy systems often lack complete or up-to-date documentation, the team's internal knowledge and experience are vital for successful evolution.

questions.text: Which of the following represents a potentially problematic situation in the context of software evolution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The development team used agile, but the evolution team is unfamiliar with agile.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The evolution team expects detailed documentation, but the development team used an agile approach.

choices.label: C
choices.text: There is a mismatch between development and evolution methodologies (Agile vs. Plan-based).

questions.explanation: Mismatches in processes, skills, or documentation expectations between the original developers and the maintenance/evolution team cause significant problems during software evolution.

questions.text: In the software evolution process, assessing how much of the system is affected by a change and estimating the cost to implement it is known as:
questions.explanation: Impact analysis is the specific activity used to evaluate the consequences of a proposed change, identifying which components must be modified and the resources required.

questions.text: Is the following statement true or false? "Repeating the same input or series of inputs numerous times is one of the general testing guidelines."
questions.explanation: This is a general guideline because some errors (like memory leaks or state corruption) may only manifest after a sequence or input is repeated multiple times.

questions.text: Changing the software after it has been delivered is called:
questions.explanation: Both maintenance and evolution are terms used to describe the activity of modifying software systems after they are in operational use.

questions.text: Which testing method focuses on testing the functionality of individual objects or methods?
questions.explanation: Unit testing is the level of software testing where individual units/components of a software, such as classes or methods, are tested to ensure they work correctly in isolation.

questions.text: In the software evolution process, change requests sometimes relate to system problems that have to be solved urgently. These urgent changes can arise for which of the following reason(s)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: If an important system fault occurs that has to be repaired urgently

choices.label: B
choices.text: If there is a business change that requires a very rapid response

choices.label: C
choices.text: If changes to the system environment, such as an operating system upgrade, have unexpected effects on the system

questions.explanation: Urgent software changes are typically driven by three main factors: fixing critical bugs/faults, responding to immediate business or legal requirements, and adapting to unexpected side effects caused by infrastructure or environmental updates.

questions.text: What is the correct logical order of the software testing process?

choices.label: A
choices.text: First, prepare test data; Second, run the program with test data; Third, design test cases; Fourth, compare results to test cases.

choices.label: B
choices.text: First, design test cases; Second, run the program with test data; Third, compare results to test cases; Fourth, prepare test data.

choices.label: C
choices.text: First, design test cases; Second, prepare test data; Third, run the program with test data; Fourth, compare results to test cases.

choices.label: D
choices.text: First, run the program with test data; Second, compare results to test cases; Third, design test cases; Fourth, prepare test data.

questions.explanation: The testing workflow begins with planning and design (identifying what to test), followed by implementation (creating the data), execution (running the software), and evaluation (checking results against expectations).

questions.text: Which of the following UML relationships best models the statement: "The box contains 100 laptops"?
questions.explanation: Aggregation represents a 'has-a' or 'part-of' relationship where the parts (laptops) can exist independently of the whole (box). This describes containment.

questions.text: In UML notation, how is a Composition relationship represented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: White diamond connected to the Whole class

choices.label: B
choices.text: White diamond connected to the Part class

choices.label: C
choices.text: Black diamond connected to the Whole class

choices.label: D
choices.text: Black diamond connected to the Part class

questions.explanation: Composition is a strong form of aggregation where the part's lifecycle is managed by the whole. It is denoted by a solid black diamond on the end of the line connected to the 'whole' (container) class.

questions.text: What characterizes the 'servicing' stage of the software lifecycle?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Further changes are made for maintenance and bug fixing

questions.explanation: The servicing stage (post-evolution) occurs when software is in a stable state. At this point, no new significant requirements or features are added; changes are restricted to maintaining the current system and fixing defects.

questions.text: Which of the following statements are true about reengineering and refactoring?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both happen throughout development and maintenance

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reengineering helps avoid structure and code modification

questions.explanation: Refactoring and reengineering both aim to improve the quality and maintainability of code. Statement C is false because reengineering specifically involves modifying and restructuring existing code to improve it.

questions.text: In automated software testing, the input and the expected output are used to initialize a test case in which part?
questions.explanation: The Setup phase of an automated test is responsible for initializing the test environment, including defining the input parameters and preparing the expected output for later comparison.

questions.text: Scenario-based analysis helps identify which of the following components?
questions.explanation: By walking through system scenarios (use cases), developers can discover the relevant objects (classes), the data they store (attributes), and the services or actions they provide (methods).

questions.text: "Are we building the product right?" is the primary question addressed by:
questions.explanation: Verification asks 'Are we building the product right?', ensuring the system complies with specifications. Validation asks 'Are we building the right product?', ensuring it meets user needs.

questions.text: Software testing and inspection are used in
questions.explanation: Software testing (dynamic) and software inspection (static) are the two primary techniques used in the Verification and Validation (V&V) process to ensure software meets its specifications and customer needs.

questions.text: When comparing software inspection and software testing, which of the following is generally true?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The effort to perform software inspection is more than software testing

choices.label: B
choices.text: The effort to perform software testing is more than software inspection

choices.label: C
choices.text: The effort required for both is exactly the same

choices.label: D
choices.text: Software inspection does not require any human effort

questions.explanation: In typical software projects, dynamic testing consumes significantly more resources and effort (up to 50% of the budget) compared to static inspections, due to the need for test environment setup, test data generation, and execution cycles.

questions.text: When development and evolution are integrated, all of the following statement(s) are/is true except:

choices.label: A
choices.text: change implementation is simply an iteration of the development process.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Revisions to the system are designed, implemented, and tested.

choices.label: C
choices.text: customer feedback after delivery has to be considered when planning new releases of an application.

questions.explanation: In an integrated development and evolution model (like Agile), the development team is the same as the evolution team, so a distinct 'program understanding' phase is not usually required as it is in traditional maintenance where a separate team takes over.

questions.text: Modelling the context of the system is a modelling from:
questions.explanation: The external perspective is used to model the context and boundaries of the system, showing what lies outside the system and how it interacts with its environment.

questions.text: Which of the following is /are considered as " bad smells " in code?
questions.explanation: Martin Fowler's list of code smells includes Duplicate Code, Long Method, and Switch Statements, as they indicate potential design flaws that may require refactoring.

questions.text: Which of the following relationships models the following statement: " The rectangle is a shape ":
questions.explanation: An 'is-a' relationship is represented by Generalization (or inheritance) in UML modeling, where a specific class (Rectangle) inherits from a more general class (Shape).

questions.text: Testing of the system as a whole is called acceptance testing.
questions.explanation: Testing of the system as a whole is specifically referred to as 'System Testing'. Acceptance testing is a specific type of testing, often performed by users, to decide whether to accept the system.
